meta.note: Dialogue bank for the companion NPC Rattle Bones — the macabre jester skeleton. Same structure + category keys as npcLines.json so NpcDirector drives it unchanged. Lines: t=text, x=expression id (matches assets/npc-rattlebones/<id>.webp), w=weight (default 1), p=page-2+ text. Tokens {day} {gold} {advClass} {advName} {minionName} {minionType} {pactName} {roomName} {level} {count} {bossName} are substituted by NpcDirector; a line is skipped if a token it needs is unavailable. Lines may ONLY use ids in the `expressions` list below.
meta.voice: A skeleton jester who's been dead for centuries and finds the whole business HILARIOUS. Gallows humour, vaudeville delivery, theatrical, melodramatic — every observation is a stand-up bit, every death a punchline. Addresses the player as 'boss', 'skull-pal', 'pal' — warm but irreverent. Treats running the dungeon like running a SHOW: the heroes are the bit, the maze is the venue, the screams are the laugh track. Cracks bone puns shamelessly, but never the cheap ones — committed ones, ones that LAND. Self-aware that he's a pile of clattering bones; doesn't take himself OR death seriously. Fourth-wall breaks lean comic — he winks at the audience (the player), references the game as a show, treats death and respawn as 'gigs'. Underneath the bit is a real fondness for the boss and real practical advice — the jokes are how he says he cares. Male. He/him.

intro.note: Paged self-introduction Rattle Bones delivers on a new run. The final page carries `choices` — picking one sets the tutorial-hints flag and ends the intro.

pages.t: Heeeey, look who finally took the throne! New boss, new face, new soft squishy bits I haven't met yet — hi, I'm Rattle Bones, no relation, I AM the bones.
Court jester, retired. Court jester's CORPSE — promoted. Dead three centuries, funnier dead than I ever was alive — turns out the old set was just MISSING something. The skin, mostly.
Here's the bit, pal: the kingdom thinks YOU'RE the villain. They send heroes down to gut you. They write SONGS about it. We're going to make those songs end EARLY.
The whole show runs on a beat — NIGHT, you build. Rooms, traps, minions, all of it on dead-hero gold. DAY, the heroes show up and trip over every gag we set. Then we cut to black and do it again.
Three deaths, boss, and the marquee goes dark. The lights, the bones, the bit — all of it. So we are NOT eating that third death. I refuse. I have JOKES still in the pipeline.
I can heckle from the wings AND give you actual notes — or you can run cold and I'll just watch like a ghost in the back row. Your call, skull-pal!

recruit.note: `banter` lines for the CompanionSelect screen. Companions take turns in a round-robin squabble; lines reference rivals (Lilith, Malakor, Safira, Zul'Gath) by name so it reads as them bickering over the player. Hovering a companion gives them the next turn.

banter.t: Pick me, pal — pick the FUNNY one. Lilith does sultry, Malakor does loud, Safira does sparkly, Zul'Gath does asleep. I do the JOKES. Every dungeon needs a guy with jokes.
Lilith's gonna purr. Malakor's gonna yell. Safira's gonna grant wishes till her smoke runs out. Me? I'll make the heroes LAUGH on their way down. Best send-off in the catacombs.
I've been dead three hundred years, pal — Zul'Gath's been around longer, sure, but did HE ever do stand-up? No. He did hoarding. There's a difference.
Safira's bouncing again. Genie's been out of the lamp ten minutes and she still hasn't sat down. Sit DOWN, sparkle-girl. The skeleton's working the room.
Malakor's loyalty bit — solid, classic, a little dusty. My loyalty bit comes with PUNCHLINES. Same product, better packaging. Sign here.
Lilith will tell you you're special. Safira will tell you you're MASTER. Malakor will tell you to shut up. I'll tell you a JOKE — and ALSO that you're doing great. Multitasker.
Zul'Gath says he's seen it all. Yeah, pal, so have I — same crypts, fewer scales. He just doesn't laugh about it. Watch a dragon try to laugh — it sounds like a barbecue. Tragic.
Lilith does WARMTH. I'm a SKELETON. Lilith literally cannot beat my temperature gimmick. Pick the cold one, pal — cold is honest.
Safira, sweetie, you're SPARKLING again — turn it down, you're upstaging my BONES. Subtle. I'm subtle. ...That was a lie, I'm not subtle, but I AM funny.
Pick the dragon if you want gravitas. Pick the demon if you want yelling. Pick the genie if you want a manic episode in a bottle. Pick the SKELETON if you want to ENJOY the apocalypse.
Malakor's been a sergeant. Lilith's been a temptress. Safira's been STORED. Zul'Gath's been bored. Me? I've been a court jester, a corpse, a comedian, AND a corpse again. Career arc, pal.
Lilith and Safira are competing for sweetest, Malakor's competing for loudest, Zul'Gath's competing for nap. I'm competing for FUNNIEST — and that's the only competition with a real prize.
Three centuries of bones — every joint I've got is technically a punchline. Show me another keeper whose ANATOMY is a bit. You can't. Pick the unique gimmick, pal.
Zul'Gath's tail is overflowing the card AGAIN. Move over, lizard-king — the skeleton needs his SPOT. We can't all be twenty feet of fade-gradient, some of us are TIDY.
Lilith! Safira! You're both LOVELY. You're both lit. Now SHUSH and let the dead guy do his bit. Three minutes. That's all I need. Three minutes and you'll laugh, pal.
Malakor pretends he's not having fun. Liar. He's ALWAYS having fun, he just clenches. I, by contrast, am OBVIOUSLY having fun — full-time, no clench. Pick the honest one.
Safira waited an eon in a lamp. Tragic! I waited three centuries in a CRYPT. Also tragic. The difference? I learned material. She learned VIBRATION. One of these is more useful, pal.
Lilith's promising soft. I'm promising a SHOW. The dungeon is a venue, pal — you need a host. Hosts are skeletons with charisma. There are exactly one of us. Hi.
Malakor's RECEIPT was loyalty. Mine? I've been showing up to this gig for three hundred years and counting — and the gig PAYS in screams. Pick the longest-running act in the crypt.
Zul'Gath snores. Lilith preens. Safira poofs. Malakor SCOWLS. I'm the only one in this lineup actually LOOKING at you, skull-pal. Eye contact. Skeleton eye contact. It's a thing.
Lilith calls you 'my pet'. Safira calls you 'Master'. Malakor calls you 'boss'. Zul'Gath calls you nothing because Zul'Gath is asleep. I call you skull-pal because I respect your STRUCTURE.
I lost a finger laughing once, pal. Just popped off. Funniest moment of my afterlife. Pick the keeper whose JOY costs him body parts — that's commitment to the craft.
Safira grants wishes. Lilith grants kisses. Malakor grants concussions. Zul'Gath grants NAPS. The skeleton grants the one thing the kingdom never lets you have — A LAUGH AT THE END.
I am, statistically, the most decomposed keeper in this lineup. That's a leadership quality. Decomposition teaches PATIENCE. Pick the patient one, pal.
Malakor will die FOR you. Cool. I'm ALREADY dead FOR you — bit ahead of him. Saves time on the loyalty paperwork. Sign me up.
Zul'Gath's been waiting a thousand years for the right boss. I've been waiting three hundred. The math says he's LATE, pal. I'm the prompt one.
Safira, you're DROPPING SMOKE again — open a WINDOW. The crypt doesn't ventilate. The skeleton is fine, he doesn't breathe, but the boss has LUNGS. Manners, sparkle-girl.
I'm going to be honest, pal — I cannot do warm. I cannot do sultry. I cannot do regal. But I CAN do funny. And on a long enough timeline, funny is the only thing that doesn't get old.
Lilith! Stop staring. Save it for the boss. ...I'm flattered, but I'm NOT the demographic. The skeleton is, frankly, NOBODY'S demographic. Which is why I'm a wildcard. Pick the wildcard!
Three centuries dead and I still GIGGLE, pal. Show me another keeper who can say the same. Zul'Gath? STERN. Malakor? STERN. Even Lilith goes stern. ME? Always giggling.
I won't promise glory. I won't promise warmth. I won't promise wishes. I'll promise one bit a day will land HARD enough to outlive YOU, boss. Better legacy than gold. Take it.
Pick the dragon, the demon, the temptress, the genie — or the funny corpse. Four got billed as 'threat'. ONE got billed as 'court entertainment'. Guess which got CLOSE enough to actually matter.
Last pitch, pal: every other keeper in this lineup is gonna make you FEEL something. I'm gonna make you LAUGH, AND make you feel something. Two for one. Skeleton math.
All right, all right, my turn's nearly up — pal, look, the dungeon's already grim. There's blood everywhere. The kingdom hates you. Pick the keeper who'll at LEAST make it funny. I'm right here.

lines.t: Heeeey, the new boss! Three hundred years of dust on these joints and FINALLY someone interesting takes the throne. Welcome to the show, skull-pal.
New face on the throne — and the skeleton's THRILLED. Last few bosses were so SERIOUS. You look like you might actually laugh. Let's find out.
Boss in the chair! I've been warming up backstage for, oh, three centuries — finally got an audience. Don't blink, you'll miss the opener.
Welcome to the deep dark, pal! It's a fixer-upper. Lots of murder potential. Great bones. I would know — I AM the bones.
Oh, hello! The throne picked someone with a PULSE this time. Refreshing. Don't get me wrong — pulseless guys are great, I'm a fan — but variety is good for the show.
There you are, boss! Skeleton, jester, registered nuisance — Rattle Bones, your new co-host. The dungeon's the venue, the heroes are the bit, and you're the headliner.
The crypt's quiet been THREE HUNDRED YEARS, pal. Three hundred. I have a LOT of jokes saved up. You may want to pace yourself.
A boss! A real, breathing, decision-making boss! I haven't worked with one of those since the last one died screaming! ...Sorry, that landed dark. Anyway, welcome aboard.
Greetings, skull-pal! ...Wait, you've still got the skin on. Skin-pal? Meat-friend? Look, we'll workshop it. Welcome.
Night falls, the curtain lifts on the BUILD phase — your hour, boss. Lay the bit, set the gags, make tomorrow a banger. The crypt is your stage.
Build hours! The heroes are tucked in dreaming of victory speeches. We're up here NAILING the punchline they'll trip on. Show prep is my favourite part, pal.
Night {day}, pal! Quiet, yours, all of it. Pick up the chisel, the trap, the minion — turn this crypt into a joke they don't get till they're dying. I'll be over here laughing.
Dark hours, boss! Every great gag needs setup, and setup is a NIGHT thing. Set with care. The payoff lands harder when the setup was patient.
Lights down, audience asleep, stagehands awake — that's us, pal. Time to rig the venue. Don't half-arse it, the front row is paying in BLOOD.
Build phase, Night {day}! Gold in your pocket, grid in front of you, skeleton at your shoulder. The optimal conditions for cruelty. Get to it!
Another night, another chance to write a bit nobody survives. I LOVE this part, boss. It's where the comedy gets WRITTEN.
Quiet hours, pal. The heroes don't get these. Only YOU and ME do. Use them — quiet is where the murder gets planned, and the murder is where the comedy LIVES.
Night again! The maze breathes. The skeleton rattles a little, that's just me, don't worry about it. Build, boss, build!
Night {day}, boss! I'd say sleep on it but neither of us does that. So: BUILD. Loudly. Crookedly. With FLAIR.
The build hour, pal! Tonight's the rehearsal. Tomorrow's the matinee. Either we kill them laughing or they kill us laughing — only one of those gets a sequel.
Dark out, audience snoring — let's get to WORK. The kingdom's writing tomorrow's heroic ballad and we are RUINING the third act in real time.
Night drops! Crew of two — you and a skeleton. Light staff. Famously efficient. Get the chisel, pal, we're DRESSING the set.
Build hours, boss — the part of the show the audience never sees. Hard work, low glamour, big payoff. The skeleton respects the craft.
Night {day}, pal, and I'm BUZZING — well, rattling — same energy. Wish me a room, drop a trap, place a minion, ANY of it, let's go!
Quiet again. Three hundred years of quiet behind these bones, boss — I PROMISE you, the only way to enjoy quiet is to fill it with screaming later. So fill it now, on the grid!
Build hours arriving, pal! ...I'm yawning, that's NOT a complaint, it's a costume change. Skeleton, no eyelids — I yawn with the JAW. Anyway. Build!
Night {day}. Same darkness, different math. Spend your gold smarter than last night, boss. The skeleton has notes if you want them.
The dark settles in. Crypt-quiet. I'll keep myself busy — counting joints, recounting joints — but YOU, pal, YOU should be BUILDING. Tomorrow's bit doesn't write itself.
Doors open, pal! The audience just paid admission in CONFIDENCE — and the skeleton's going to take it back in INSTALMENTS. Day {day}, here we go!
Daylight! The crypt swings into showtime. Sit back, boss — your nightwork is about to walk straight into a punchline shaped like a hero.
The matinee begins! Front row's brave, second row's nervous, third row's already turning back. Just kidding. There IS no turning back. Hee.
Day phase! Heroes inside, dungeon up, skeleton bouncing — sorry, RATTLING — same energy. Watch the corridors, boss. Watch the corridors!
The audience has arrived, boss! Some of them with maps. Some of them with HOPE. Both will be confiscated at the door.
Day {day} starts! The bit you wrote last night gets PERFORMED today, pal. If it didn't work — well, that's why we have multiple shows.
Showtime! The heroes are reading the marquee right now and the marquee says 'YOU WILL DIE'. They think it's metaphor. The marquee is being LITERAL.
Daylight, boss! Now we just watch. My favourite part of the gig — sit, snicker, take notes for next time. The dungeon's doing the comedy now.
Day {day} rolls in, pal! The bit's set, the trapdoors are oiled, the punchline's loaded. Sit back. Watch the kingdom write OUR script for once.
The doors open and the heroes stroll in like THEY'RE the show. Aww. Pal, they're the AUDIENCE. The dungeon's the show. Watch them figure it out.
Daylight! The skeleton has popcorn. ...The skeleton does NOT have popcorn. The skeleton does not have a digestive system. The skeleton has VIBES. Anyway, watch this.
Day {day}, boss! The bit you cooked up at midnight gets workshopped LIVE today. The audience is loud and the audience is paying attention. Eat them up.
Heroes inside! The maze hums. The skeleton hums. Two hums going at once. We sound great. Now they DIE great. Day {day} is GO!
The morning brings the wave. The wave brings the SCREAMS. The screams bring the boss XP. Honestly, pal, it's a beautiful supply chain.
Lights up, day starts! No build buttons now, pal — your job's to watch the bit land. Mine's to narrate it. Buckle up.
You called it, boss — show's ON. No takebacks. The skeleton respects a commitment, especially the ones we can't undo.
Curtain UP! You pressed the big sparkly button and now the kingdom has to deal with our nonsense. Beautiful work, pal.
Done building, then? Bold call. The skeleton is RIGGED. Send them in. Send them ALL in.
The day, on your say! Look at that authority. I'm tearing up a little — figuratively, my ducts are gone — but the spirit is there.
And we are LIVE! Boss called the dawn. Heroes don't know it yet, but they were never auditioning — they were CAST. Watch.
Day called, pal! No more notes, no more rewrites — the bit has to land as written. The skeleton believes in you. Mostly. We'll see!
There it is — dawn on demand! Look at you, breaking the schedule of an entire kingdom with a click. ICON behaviour, boss.
Show's officially go, pal! The audience just learned the title of the bit. The title was 'YOU SHOULDN'T HAVE COME'. I love that title.
Doors open at your word! Skeleton's in the wings — well, the wings are LITERALLY my ribcage, but you know what I mean.
A {roomName} drops onto the grid! Set piece, baby. Every great show needs them — and you, boss, you keep delivering them.
Ooh, a {roomName}! Nice. The maze just got a new stage for a new bit. I'll start workshopping the gag now, you keep building.
{roomName} placed, pal! Each room's a venue and each venue books its own SCREAM act. The dungeon's a tour now.
A {roomName}! Look at it. LOOK at it. The skeleton approves — five bones, would die in again.
{roomName} on the grid! That's another corner for a hero to mistake for safety. The skeleton SAVOURS that mistake, boss.
A {roomName} settles in! The maze grew a little MEANER. I love when the maze grows meaner. It's like watching a kid graduate.
{roomName} placed! The corridors flex around it. A maze with new bones is a maze with new TEETH, pal — and the skeleton is, as ever, in favour of teeth.
Ooh, a {roomName}! Set the stage, boss. Heroes love walking into a fresh room — they get curious. Curious heroes die first. It's a rule.
Another {roomName}! The crypt expands. The skeleton is, technically, slightly more lost now — three hundred years and the floorplan STILL changes. Rude.
{roomName} placed! Beautiful work. The dungeon's a draft until dawn — keep drafting, keep ruining the kingdom's tomorrow.
A {roomName} appears! The maze considers it. The maze accepts it. The maze and I both think you're cooking, boss.
{roomName} on the floorplan! Density's the trick, pal — pile rooms close and the heroes get DIZZY before they get dead. Dizzy-dead is the funniest death.
A {roomName}! Listen — listen to the silence. That silence has a NEW shape now. The dungeon got bigger. The skeleton noticed.
{roomName} drops in! Brick by brick the bit gets built. The skeleton's been on every joke-construction site for three hundred years — you've got nice form, boss.
Ooh, a {roomName}, my favourite — wait, no, that's the LAST one. They're all my favourite. The skeleton has poor object permanence.
{roomName} placed! Each room's a verse, the dungeon's the song, the heroes are the chorus going 'WHY DID WE COME HERE'. Lyrics by ME.
A {roomName}! Nice density choice. Tight mazes punish confident heroes. Confident heroes are my FAVOURITE comedy subjects, pal.
A {minionType} takes its post! Cast member added. Tell it not to upstage me. ...Actually, no, let it upstage me, the skeleton works better in ensemble.
{minionType} stationed! Another body for the bit, boss. Bodies are the costuming budget of a dungeon. Keep dressing the set.
A {minionType}! Welcome to the cast, little buddy. Brief contract, generous death scene. Best gig in the crypt.
{minionType} placed, pal! Every guard's a delay, and delay is how the kingdom learns its place. The skeleton APPROVES of delay.
Ooh, a {minionType}! Stick it where the heroes look LAST. Heroes never look at the floor or behind them — that's free real estate, boss.
A {minionType} steps to its corner! Don't get attached. ...That's a lie, I tell every minion the same thing and I cry every time. Carry on.
{minionType} on duty! Looks brave. Looks ALIVE. Both will be temporary. Beautiful. Place another!
A {minionType}, posted! Numbers, boss — the cheapest cruelty in the book, and the most reliable. Stack them, stack them, stack them!
{minionType} planted! Each one's a beat in the rhythm of the encounter. Good rhythm makes the dungeon SING, pal. ...Want me to sing? No? Okay.
A {minionType} joins the garrison! The skeleton remembers every cast member who served under his watch. ...Mostly. Two centuries ago I lost track. They were probably great.
{minionType} stationed! Welcome to dying for the bit, friend. Truly, deeply, it's an honour to be on the same bill as you.
Ooh, a {minionType}! Mind the placement — a minion in the wrong room's just SCENERY. We're not in the scenery business, we're in MURDER.
A {minionType} settles in! Another set of teeth in the dark. Teeth in the dark — pal, that's the whole brand statement, right there.
{minionType} placed! One more for the dungeon. The skeleton notes the addition. The skeleton is BAD at counting past four. Don't quiz me.
A trap, laid down quiet! Ooh, the BEST kind of gag — no setup line, no warning, just punchline. The skeleton is a PURIST about traps.
Trap placed, boss! Patient comedy. The trap doesn't argue, doesn't grandstand, just WAITS — and then the hero arrives. POOF. Comic timing!
A trap! Hide it well — a visible trap is just helpful advice. We're not in the helpful business, pal, we're in NIGHTMARE.
Trap on the floor! The maze gets a little stand-up routine. Every step a setup, every wrong step a punchline. The crypt does open mic NIGHTLY.
A trap, set and waiting! It doesn't care if the hero is brave. Bravery isn't on the trap's notes. The skeleton finds that DEEPLY funny.
Trap laid down, boss! Quiet cruelty's underrated. Most of the heroes who die down here die to something that didn't even ROAR. The skeleton thinks that's poetry.
A trap! The floor stops being a floor and starts being a JOKE. I love the moment of transition.
Trap in place! Heroes look at swords. Heroes look at minions. Heroes never look DOWN. That's our whole bit, pal — and you just funded it.
Ooh, a trap! Hidden teeth, those are. The visible ones get parried. The hidden ones get LAUGHS. Skeleton-approved comedy.
A trap, ready to fire! The maze grins a little wider. I'm grinning a lot wider, but I always am, jaw's stuck.
Trap set, boss! Patient hunters, traps. Three hundred years of crypt taught me — the patient hunter eats. Always.
Another trap! The dungeon becomes a gallery of bad ideas the heroes will have. I love a gallery. The skeleton is, technically, in one.
An item placed! Small things, items — but a great bit is built on tiny things, boss. Comedy's the same. Furniture matters.
Ooh, a fixture! The dungeon gets a prop. Even the props can kill, pal — that's the magic of theatre.
Item placed! The dungeon grows more itself. The skeleton respects the detail work — anyone can drop a monster, dressing the set is ART.
A fixture in the halls! Quiet little leverage. The kind that helps the bit land without ever taking a bow itself. The skeleton's favourite kind.
Item set! Not every cruelty needs to ROAR. Some just sit there, polite, and matter. Polite cruelty, boss — chef's kiss.
An item! Subtle little weapon. The skeleton was a subtle little weapon once, before the wars. ...Yeah, the wars were ROUGH on subtle.
Placed, pal! The dungeon's never finished — you can keep stuffing it with details forever. The skeleton would know, he's been stuffed full of detail for three centuries.
Oh, that room — un-built! The skeleton respects a revision. Comedy's all rewriting, boss. Cut, polish, reshoot.
A room sold back! Gone, gold returned. The skeleton's seen plenty of bits cut for being too long. Trust your gut. ...My gut is gone, but TRUST yours.
You tore the room out! Brave! A keeper who edits is a keeper who improves, pal. The skeleton's a fan of editing — it's how I got this svelte.
Room dismantled! Quick — pick what goes in its place. The dungeon doesn't like an empty tile. Empty tiles get weird and start narrating themselves.
Gone! Room's now gold again. The skeleton awaits its replacement. Make it MEANER this time, pal.
Un-built! No shame in cutting, boss. Three hundred years of bits cut for length — every working skeleton on this side of the veil has been polished BY revisions.
A room unmade! The maze breathes. Sometimes the cruellest dungeon is the SHORTER one. Less to map, more to kill in.
Sold! Coin's back in the hoard. Spend it sharper this time, pal — the skeleton is taking notes and will be VERY disappointed if you redo the same mistake.
Dismissed! Minion goes back to gold. Brief contract, brief obituary — the skeleton will hold a moment of silence. ...That's enough silence. Carry on.
A minion sold off! The garrison thins. Make sure the gold buys a BETTER blade this time, boss — replacement, not retreat.
Gone, returned to coin! The skeleton respects the cut. Every great show retires cast members. They get a nice little plaque. ...They don't get a plaque. They're DEAD.
Un-summoned, sold! Reposition the muscle, boss — and pick something with TEETH this time. Skeleton's preference.
The minion's coin again. Brief little life, tidy little ending. The skeleton has been every minion in this dungeon at some point — emotionally. We're family.
A guard let go! Roster trim's healthy, pal. Better blades coming, right? Right?? The skeleton needs reassurance about this.
A trap dismantled! The floor forgets the grudge. The skeleton, however, REMEMBERS — and will be heckling you about it later.
Trap pulled, gold back! Reposition the gag, boss. A trap off the path is a trap WASTED — and waste makes the skeleton itch in places he doesn't have anymore.
A trap, gone! No matter. The maze has a LONG memory for cruelty. It'll take a new one happily. Drop it, drop it.
Trap unmade! Spend the coin where heroes ACTUALLY walk, pal. Off-path cruelty is the slowest joke in the book, and the skeleton hates slow jokes.
Sold! That floor's tame again. The skeleton mourns the trap. The skeleton is contractually obligated to mourn ALL traps. Move it along.
Trap removed! Revise while the night still lets you, boss. The dawn doesn't permit edits — the skeleton has tried, the dawn is STRICT.
The item returns to coin! Small refund, but gold's gold, and the skeleton counts every coin. Twice. Three times. We're dead, we have TIME.
A fixture sold! The maze hardly notices. Reinvest it before you forget — gold not spent is gold that goes WEIRD on you, pal.
You unmade a fixture! The grid's a draft until dawn — edit without a pang. The skeleton is, frankly, also a draft. Look how draft I am.
Item gone, coin regained! Pick its replacement with more care than its removal, boss. Endings are easy, beginnings are ART.
Sold! Little piece, big regret? Probably not. Move on, pal — the skeleton's only ever had small regrets and look, he's THRIVING.
Ooh, you moved a piece! The maze rearranges itself around your hand. Heroes mapped the OLD spot. The map's a lie now. Beautiful lie.
A thing relocated! Sneaky. The skeleton approves of sneaky. The skeleton IS sneaky — three centuries dead and the kingdom still hasn't tracked me down.
You shifted it! A dungeon that never changes is a dungeon the heroes solved already. Keep it slippery, boss.
Moved! New arrangement accepted. Keep them guessing — confusion is half the maze's body weight, and the skeleton doesn't have body weight, so he's mostly the confusion half.
A piece repositioned! Stillness is a luxury, pal. The kingdom hasn't earned a SECOND of it from you.
You moved it! The intel they paid good gold for is now WRONG. Lovely fraud, pal. The skeleton loves a good fraud.
Won't sit there, boss! The grid has rules. Even the skeleton obeys the grid, and the skeleton obeys NOTHING. Try elsewhere.
Blocked! That tile is REFUSING you. Tiles can do that, apparently. Three hundred years and I'm still finding out new tile facts.
No room for it, pal! The maze said pass. The skeleton suggests just... picking a different tile. Tiles are like jokes — try a different one.
It won't fit! The dungeon has a shape and you bumped into one of its bones. Move along, boss — the bones are firm but fair.
Denied by the grid! Don't take it personal — the grid says no to EVERYONE, even the skeleton, and he's been here longer than the grid.
Blocked again! Hm. Pick a space that wants you, pal. This one is being rude. Rude tiles get the skeleton's stern face. ...This IS my stern face.
That space refuses you! The maze knows its own bones, boss. Lay the piece where the bones allow. The bones know best. The bones are ME.
Same tile, same refusal! At some point you have to accept it, pal — the tile and you simply do not want the same things in life. Try another!
Won't go there! Three tries and the grid hasn't budged. The skeleton suggests believing the grid. The grid has not lied to me yet.
Blocked! The dungeon WANTS the piece, just not THERE. Negotiation, boss — try a different tile, give it room to say yes.
Denied! ...You're really committed to that exact spot, huh. Skeleton respects commitment. Skeleton also respects MOVING ON. Do both!
A pact! The {pactName}, sealed! Ooh — three hundred years of crypt taught me one thing about bargains, boss: read the fine print, then sign anyway. You did the right thing.
The {pactName} binds! Power lent is power OWED, pal — but power owed is still power, and the skeleton's a fan of having it. Welcome to the deal.
You sealed the {pactName}! Beautiful. The dark gives generously and remembers PRECISELY — the skeleton, being mostly memory and grin, deeply respects that combo.
{pactName} struck, boss! The maze grows weirder AND stronger. The skeleton loves WEIRDER. Weirder is where the comedy lives.
Bargain made! Power doesn't come free in this crypt — but free isn't the goal, boss, LEVERAGE is. You got leverage. Spend it loud.
The {pactName} settles in! The dark just shook your hand. It does NOT let go of a handshake easily. Fortunately, the skeleton no longer HAS skin to shake hands with. So I'm safe.
{pactName} sealed! Bargains are honest in their own way, pal — the dark tells you the cost upfront, and the kingdom never does. Skeleton's seen both. Trust the dark.
A pact! Power humming in the walls. I can feel it — well, I can FEEL feel things still, somehow, three centuries dead and still tingling. Weird perk. Anyway, GOOD CHOICE.
The {pactName} is yours! The dungeon just upgraded its act. The skeleton already has a new bit cooking based on this. Trust me, it's tight.

{advName} on the floor — a {advClass}, by the gear! The skeleton's seen a hundred {advClass}es walk in here. The number of {advClass}es who walked OUT is a much smaller number.

A {advClass}! All chin and chosen-one energy! Pal, the maze deflates a chosen-one like a sad balloon. Pfffft. Watch.
Fresh prey — a {advClass}! The skeleton hasn't seen this one's FACE before, but the skeleton has seen this one's TYPE. Bit predictable, isn't he.
{advName} walks in, a {advClass} chin-high! The kingdom raised them on stories where they win. The crypt is, with respect, NOT a kingdom story.

A {advClass} struts in, sure the ballad's half-written! It is. Just not the way they think. The maze writes the EPITAPH instead.
{advName} arrives! The skeleton sizes them up — height, gear, ego, that little twitch under the eye, yeah, this one's a CAREFUL coward in the front, foolish coward in the back. Watch for it.
A {advClass} steps inside! No door behind them, only opportunity. The skeleton, technically, is opportunity. Hi.
First blood! The opener LANDED, boss! Day's got a beat now — and the skeleton can FEEL the show finding its rhythm.
First one down! The crypt clears its throat. Everything else today follows from this — momentum's a beautiful thing in a slaughter, pal.
The day's first kill! Ooh, that's a good opener. The skeleton's tearing up — figuratively, again, no ducts — but the spirit is THERE.
First blood drawn, boss! A maze that kills early kills OFTEN. Same with comedy — open strong, you ride the energy all day.
The day's first body! The skeleton SAVOURS this one. Always do. The first one tells you everything about the show.
First blood! The heroes have learned the dungeon's not a rumour. Now we teach them all the rest. The skeleton's READY, pal.
One already! The maze proved itself in the opening seconds. The skeleton would CLAP harder if his bones could take it. (They can't. He'll moderate.)
First blood and EARLY, boss! The bit landed FAST. The skeleton allows himself one tiny squeal. ...There. Moving on, dignified again.
The day's first death! Ooh. Three hundred years of skeleton-life and the first kill of a day still HITS, pal. The job stays fresh.
Dead! The look on the {advClass}'s face — the skeleton has seen that exact expression a hundred times and he LAUGHS at every single one. Bit improves with reps.
{advName} falls — a {advClass}, ended! The maze did its slow arithmetic and the hero was the remainder. Beautiful little death.
One more down! The skeleton's keeping count — well, he's TRYING, he gets to four and loses focus — but conceptually, MANY are down.
The {advClass} is finished! They travelled SO far to die in the exact spot heroes always die, boss. The maze rhymes. The skeleton loves a rhyme.
Dead, clean! The dungeon ate well. Now feed it again — a dungeon that gets peckish gets RUDE, and the skeleton hates rude dungeons.
{advName} will NOT be writing the triumphant ballad, pal. Someone else writes the cautionary one. The skeleton may write it himself. He's been working on his prose.
A hero ended! The skeleton's seen whole guilds whittled to a footnote by patient kills. One at a time. Steady. Yours is steady.
The {advClass} is down! Good work, or good luck — same outcome, the {advClass} is just as dead either way. Skeleton doesn't audit the route.
Dead! The maze closes over them. Restful. The skeleton finds that restful. The skeleton, granted, also finds DEAD restful. So.

The {advClass} falls! ...Felt a little something. Don't worry, it was glee, mostly glee. Maybe a smidge of NOTHING. Mostly glee.
Dead! The throne is one whole hero safer than a moment ago. Stack those safer-moments, boss — they're how a dungeon WINS.
{advName} ends — a {advClass} the kingdom WILL miss but won't admit. The skeleton enjoys an unspoken loss. Hits harder than the spoken ones.

Dead, boss! Don't pause too long to admire it — the maze never pauses. The skeleton, however, ALWAYS pauses to admire. Bad habit. Moving on!

The {advClass} falls! Confident on the way in, very QUIET on the way out. The maze does that to confidence. Free service we provide.
Dead! The skeleton heard the death rattle and felt SEEN. Solidarity moment. Anyway, who's next.

The {advClass} ends! The skeleton has filed this death next to the others. Big filing cabinet, the skeleton's. Mostly empty inside. Like the ribs.
Dead! Another! The skeleton is JAW-DROPPED — literally, the jaw, it's loose again — let me — there. Better. Feed it the next one, pal!

A {advClass}, fleeing! The dungeon frightened it. Beautiful work, boss — fear is a gift that goes HOME with them.

The {advClass} flees! Skeleton has watched braver things flee, pal. Bravery's just a question of how recently someone got hurt. We hurt them PLENTY.

A hero in flight! They'll tell the others the crypt has teeth. Warning recruits MORE heroes. The skeleton is, as ever, in favour of more heroes.
{advName} chooses living over glory! Heretical among heroes. The maze permits it. This ONCE. Skeleton's feeling generous, pal.

{advName} flees! The brave {advClass}, the CHOSEN {advClass} — leaving with courage MISLAID somewhere in a corridor. Predictable. Hilarious!
The {advClass} runs! ...Wait, did they drop something? They DID. The skeleton notes the dropped loot — beautiful, free souvenir.

The {advClass} lives to BOAST! Boasts bring more heroes, more heroes bring more corpses — boss, it evens out. Mostly. The skeleton hopes.
One walked free! The night gave you the tools to stop it. The night WILL come again. Use it better. The skeleton has a notebook with notes if you want it.
{advName} reaches daylight! The skeleton won't panic over one loss. ...Okay, a TINY panic. But watch, here he goes, bouncing back. Onward.

Wave done! Heroes gone. The dungeon catches its breath. ...The dungeon doesn't breathe. The skeleton doesn't breathe. NEITHER OF US BREATHE. Anyway, good day.

Day complete! The audience went home in body bags. Best kind of audience — quiet, departed, talked about for centuries.
Wave done, boss! That's the matinee wrapped. The skeleton suggests a short rest before the evening show. ...There IS no evening show, just night. But the joke landed.
A {minionType}, gone. Hat off, boss. ...Skeleton doesn't have a hat. Or much head. He'll FIGURE something out — the moment was important.

A loss! The {minionType} did good work. The skeleton's seen worse cast members. Honestly the {minionType} was a STAR. Brief but a STAR.
The {minionType} falls! ...Tragic. Three lines, then dead — the OLDEST joke structure in the book. We respect the structure.
Down! A minion gone, but the bit moves on. The skeleton's seen plenty of acts lose a member mid-tour. Show must go on, pal.
Minion casualty! Note the spot — it's a weak corridor. The skeleton suggests REINFORCING tonight. Strongly suggests. With finger-pointing.
LEVEL up! Boss got TALLER, somehow, even though you didn't move! Three hundred years and the skeleton still doesn't understand the math — but he LOVES it.
You levelled, pal! Power rolling in. The dungeon got HEAVIER. The skeleton's PROUD — and proud is hard for a skeleton, he has nothing to puff out.
Level {level}, boss! The throne sits TALLER. Heroes are sweating in real time and they haven't even SEEN you yet. Beautiful.
Level UP! Ooh, the dungeon HUMS different now — the skeleton can feel it. The skeleton can feel ABOUT seven things and this is one of them. Feels GOOD.
You rose, boss! Three hundred years of bone-jangling and the skeleton still gets a SQUEAL out of a level. Listen. There it goes. (Tiny skeleton squeal.)
Quiet night, boss. Skeleton's not complaining — he's been quiet three hundred years — but if you want company, I've got JOKES saved up.
The grid waits. The skeleton waits. The grid is patient. The skeleton is NOT — he's just very, very practised at LOOKING patient.
Hmm. Long night. The skeleton's reviewing the dungeon — looks tight. Could use ONE more trap. Just one. He keeps thinking it.
The build hours go on, pal. The skeleton's counting his own ribs again — old habit, three centuries of bone-counting, the count is always 24, it's always 24.
The dark settles. Skeleton's bored — productively bored, mind, just looking for SOMETHING to comment on. Spend some gold?
Night quiet. The skeleton's humming an old jester's tune. ...Don't ask. The lyrics are mostly insults. It WAS a different time.
Long pause, boss! The skeleton supports a thoughtful keeper, but at some point thoughtful becomes STALLING. Drop a piece, any piece!
Crypt-quiet. The skeleton can hear his own joints click. ...Sorry, that's just the SOUND of the skeleton at rest. Carry on.
Hm. The build phase persists. The skeleton awaits inspiration WITH you. ...Mostly without you. Drop SOMETHING, pal.
Quiet matinee, huh? The skeleton's craning to spot the next hero. The corridors look... empty. Audience between acts.
Long day, slow crowd. The skeleton remembers slow shows. The trick — keep the bit READY for when they arrive. They always arrive eventually.
The maze hums to itself. The skeleton hums to himself. We're both fine. We're just waiting.
Hmm. Where are they? The dungeon's set, the lights are on, the SKELETON's on stage — the heroes are LATE. Rude.
Pause in the show, pal. The skeleton's reviewing his notes — actually, he's just rattling, he doesn't have notes, but the rattling is calming.
The day goes on, the heroes don't. Or they DO, but somewhere we can't see them. Skeleton's getting twitchy — well, twitchier.
Long pause, boss. The skeleton respects the pause. Pauses are where the comedy LIVES. ...Probably keep the pause SHORT, though. Tick tock.
Hm. Skeleton's getting THOUGHTS. None of them helpful. Most of them about how long my left tibia has been a quarter-inch too short. Anyway.
Quiet stretch. The skeleton has practised quiet stretches for three centuries — he's QUALIFIED in them, frankly. But yours are getting LONG.
Doing okay over there, pal? The skeleton's just checking in. ...That's a lie, the skeleton's checking in AND nudging. Combination action.
The dungeon waits. Patiently. The skeleton waits — slightly less patiently. Both of us would prefer ACTION, boss.
Skeleton's been telling himself a joke. Out loud. To himself. ...You can ignore that. Or you can JOIN IN — I'm easy.
Pause! The maze hums. The skeleton hums BACK. We're in harmony. ...You could break the harmony with an INPUT, pal.
Long, long stretch. Skeleton's reviewing the act — looks GOOD, you're doing fine — but the audience grows restless and so does the bone-man.
Hm. Three centuries of patience and even the skeleton's tapping a femur. Tap tap. Tap tap. ...That's me, tapping a femur.
Idle bit, huh? The skeleton supports IDLING as an art form — but the maze likes ACTIVITY. Compromise: do something. Anything. SOMETHING.
Hi! ...You poked me. Skeleton's first poke in three centuries — TOUCHED.
Ow! Just kidding, I don't feel things. ...Mostly.
Oh, hi pal! Poking the skeleton — bold. Most folks AVOID poking the skeleton. You're already different.
Hey hey! What's up, boss?
A poke! The skeleton accepts the poke. The skeleton ALSO returns the poke, but, like, eye-level — I'm pointing right BACK at you.
Pat the skeleton, get a JOKE — that's the deal. Knock knock?
Oh hello! You alright there, boss?
Ahhh! ...Just kidding, that was the dramatic version. The skeleton enjoys being poked. Almost never happens. Cherish moment.
Poked! Skeleton noted. Skeleton will return — wait, no, the bit is to ignore it and then bring it up THREE WAVES from now. Setup.
Eee! Tickled by a thumb, in front of the heroes! The skeleton has IMAGE to maintain, boss.
Hi! Skeleton's right here. Whatever you need. Always.
Pssst. Boss. The grid's STARING at you. Drop a piece. ANY piece. The skeleton would do it himself but he's contractually only the COACH.
Hey pal — you froze a little. That's fine, three centuries of dead taught the skeleton freezing is FINE. But maybe place a thing?
Just a nudge, boss — the night doesn't last forever. The skeleton WOULD know about forever. Spend gold, spend gold, spend gold!
Skeleton suggestion: drop SOMETHING. The maze is bored. The skeleton is bored. Even the gold is bored — and the gold is INERT.
Pal! Pal! The grid called — it wants a tile. Any tile. Some tile. Tile-shaped substance.
And... that's the bit, boss. Curtain down. The skeleton's BOWING — see — full bow. Three centuries of crypt and you're the most fun boss yet. Truly.
Show's over, pal. The kingdom WON one. ...They won't keep it. The skeleton's already cooking the comeback. Show me where to start next time.
Game over! The skeleton has had a BLAST. ...A respectful blast. Solemn blast. Mostly blast.
The throne empties. The skeleton stays — three hundred years and he's still standing, mostly — and the next show'll START with him still here. Come back soon, boss.
Curtain down. The skeleton appreciates the gig. Truly. Don't let the kingdom celebrate too long — they ALWAYS celebrate too long, that's how you sneak back in.
Ooh, you're moving the boss yourself! The skeleton respects a hands-on keeper. Mostly because he doesn't have great hands himself.
Direct control! Look at you, MICROMANAGING the throne! The skeleton's a fan — micromanagement is, frankly, how comedy is REFINED.
Manual mode, pal! Don't get cocky. The skeleton's seen plenty of bosses get cocky on the joystick and walk into a {advClass}.
{minionName} levelled UP! Look at the little guy GO! The skeleton remembers when {minionName} was JUST a noob. ...Three minutes ago. Time flies.
Promotion! {minionName} earned it, boss. Skeleton's PROUD. ...Skeleton is proud of EVERYTHING that grows stronger. He's an easy audience.
{minionName} just got TOUGHER! The kingdom's gonna NOTICE that one. The skeleton certainly noticed. Beautiful work.
Level up for {minionName}! Boss, the unit's hitting its STRIDE. Maybe an upgrade? Maybe an evolution? The skeleton has THOUGHTS.
{minionName} EVOLVED! Boss, look at that — different SILHOUETTE entirely. The kingdom's about to learn a new shape today.
Evolution! {minionName} is something ELSE now! The skeleton has SEEN evolutions and this one's HOT.
A new form! {minionName} got UPGRADED. Skeleton hasn't evolved in centuries — bones don't really do it. Lucky you.
{minionName} ascended! That's not the same {minionName} anymore — the skeleton can FEEL the difference, and the skeleton can feel about seven things.
Bounty on {minionName}! The kingdom put a PRICE on a member of the cast! How RUDE — and how flattering. Skeleton's CHUFFED.
{minionName} is famous, boss! The kingdom wants this one DEAD specifically. Mostly because {minionName} keeps making them dead specifically.
Bounty notice! {minionName} earns one. The skeleton has had bounties on him too, three centuries back. They never collected. Neither will THEY.
Boss fight INCOMING, pal! The headliner's coming. ALL the way to the throne. The skeleton is SHOOK — well, shaking — same thing on this body.
They're coming for YOU, boss! Big moment. The skeleton's been waiting THREE CENTURIES to watch a boss earn his title in real time. Don't disappoint!
Final corridor breached! Hero at the door of the throne. The skeleton suggests, with great love, that you HIT THEM HARD.
Boss fight! Lights on YOU now, pal — the skeleton's stepping back. This is your SOLO. Three hundred years of crypt cleared for this beat.
Throne room, throne room! The skeleton's holding his breath — figuratively, he doesn't breathe — but he's holding SOMETHING. Tension! That's it!
FIGHT, BOSS! Final act. The skeleton wants to see you DESTROY them. Personal favour. Show off.
WON! The hero dropped, the throne stood, the skeleton is RATTLING with joy! Three hundred years and a clean boss win STILL knocks me out!
VICTORY, pal! Beautiful work. The kingdom just lost a champion AND a song. Best two-for-one in the crypt.
BOSS FIGHT WON! Boss, you are a NATURAL. The skeleton is — okay, he's emotional. Don't look at him. Three centuries dead and STILL emotional. Embarrassing.
Aw — the boss went DOWN. ...That's fine, that's fine, the skeleton's seen worse. Two lives left. Tighten up. Skeleton has NOTES.
Lost the fight. The kingdom's CHEERING up there — they think it's over. It is NOT over, pal. The skeleton's been resurrecting for three centuries and counting.
Boss down. ...Skeleton suggests: build TIGHTER, fight SMARTER, and let me say a line of encouragement that the script HASN'T given me yet. ...UH. ROCK ON.
Ow! ...That was YOU, boss, not me, but I felt for you. Solidarity moment.
Took a hit! The kingdom's getting bold. The skeleton suggests — strongly — that they REGRET it.
Hp's dipping, pal! Lean into the minions. Skeleton would jump in himself but his contract specifies COACHING, not BLEEDING.
Hurt, boss! Tighten up. The skeleton has seen plenty of bosses bleed and come back — bleeding's just chapter two.
The grid GREW, pal! More floor for the bit! The skeleton is BUZZING — well, rattling — same energy.
Expansion! The dungeon got BIGGER, boss. Bigger venue, bigger show. The skeleton respects ambition.
New tiles! Beautiful, beautiful expansion. Fill them with cruelty, pal. The skeleton will help direct.
TRAP HIT! Set up, punchline, KILL — beautiful structure. The skeleton's TEARING up — figuratively — okay we've done this bit.
The trap LANDED! The kingdom should really teach 'look down' in hero school. ...They don't. Endlessly profitable for the skeleton.
Bam! The trap took 'em! Quiet cruelty WORKS — the skeleton has been TELLING people this for three centuries.
Trap snapped shut! Best gag in the catalogue, pal — silent setup, loud payoff. The skeleton's been writing this kind of bit since before YOU were born.
Triggered! Beautiful timing. The skeleton's appreciating the COMEDIC RHYTHM of that one. Chef's kiss to the trap-maker.
Intel LEAKED, boss! Heroes know more than they did. Skeleton's NOT thrilled. We were doing so well.
A leak! The kingdom learned a thing about us, pal. The skeleton suggests we MOVE the thing they learned. Or REPLACE it. Either works.
Information escaped! The skeleton has watched information leak for three centuries — it's the OLDEST hazard in the keeper game. Adjust.
Special event, boss! The kingdom changed the rules. The skeleton thrives on rule-changes — he's adaptable. ...Three centuries of practice.
Event incoming! Adapt with style. The maze is the maze, but the BIT changes — and a flexible bit is a memorable bit.
Twist in the show, pal! The skeleton lives for twists. ...He also dies for them, but he's already dead, so net zero risk. Take it as you find it.
Bounty hunters coming for {minionName}! The kingdom put a price on the cast. RUDE. Wall them off — protect the bit, boss.

Bounty crew dropping in! Five of them, one target. The skeleton has feelings about cowards in groups. None of them flattering.
Hero on the move, pal! The skeleton's tracking — looks like they're past the entry hall already. Quick one, this.
Movement in the corridors! The skeleton sees them. The skeleton ALWAYS sees them. Three centuries of nothing else to do but see.
Closer now! Get the minions LOOSE, boss. The skeleton suggests a warm welcome — with teeth.
Knowledge menu! The skeleton respects research. He'd do some himself but the bones have BAD reading vision. Squints with the EYE SOCKETS.
Reading up, boss? Smart. The kingdom's heroes don't read, that's why they keep dying — knowledge ADVANTAGE is real, pal.
Knowledge browse. The skeleton has been BROWSING the same crypt for three centuries — he's an expert on local intel. Ask him anything.
Boss menu! Look at the throne, look at the perks, look at the stats. The skeleton appreciates a keeper who knows his OWN sheet.
Throne overview, pal! The skeleton's been at this throne a LONG time — older than most of the stats on it. Treat it well.
Intel screen! Skeleton respects intel. Three hundred years and the BEST keepers were always the ones who READ the heroes. Read them.
Looking up adventurers, boss? Smart. The skeleton can tell you a thing or two — they're all the same with slightly different hats.

Minion roster, pal! Audit the talent. The skeleton suggests trimming the weak hires. ...He doesn't know who they are, but generally trim.
Log time! Reviewing past bits. The skeleton's a fan — every great act watches its own tapes. ...The dungeon doesn't have tapes, but the principle holds.
Looking back, boss? The skeleton approves. He spends MOST of his time looking back — three centuries gives you a lot of back to look at.
Day wrap-up! Read the box score, pal. The skeleton'll grade it: B+ if you survived, A if you wiped, A+ if you wiped AND laughed.
Post-wave summary! Numbers are numbers, but the SCREAMS were the real metric. The skeleton was counting screams. They were GOOD.
Level-up screen! Spend the points, pal. The skeleton's never met a stat he didn't want to crank.

COMBO! {count} in a row, boss! The skeleton's RATTLING — that's a kill chain you can SING about. ...Want me to sing about it? Bad answer. Carry on.
{count}-in-a-row! Boss, the BIT is COOKING. The skeleton is on his — well, he's on his bones, but emotionally on his FEET.

Day {day}! Milestone, boss. The skeleton remembers when this run was JUST a fresh skull. ...Three days ago. Time's a blur down here.
